markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Write the spec-ready contract for UserManual as a durable product feature and build deliverable replacing legacy ModelManual wording. This MT belongs to MasterSpecEnrichmentSeed. Prepare the future Master Spec refinement/enrichment payload without relying on sidecar research.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Prepare spec-enrichment-ready wording without editing the Master Spec in this stub task.
Where current Master Spec text is stale, mark the exact stale contract and the replacement Handshake-native rule.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualSpecSeed leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualSpecSeed is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: AC1 (UserManualSpecSeed specified for no-context continuation) -> the spec-ready UserManual contract is applied law in .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.192/spec-modules/10-product-surfaces.md#10.15.8 UserManual migration bridge: UserManual is the canonical product concept; legacy model_manual modules/IPC/tests/projections are compatibility paths that must map deterministically to UserManualRecord entries and emit compatibility receipts; UserManualRecord MUST explain purpose, workflows, startup, run commands, inputs/outputs, navigation, safety constraints, failure modes, recovery steps, visual-debug/backend-navigation hooks; new WP-KERNEL-009 surfaces MUST update UserManual in the same implementation unit (build-rule defect otherwise); reinforced by 02-system-architecture.md#2.3.13.11 UserManualRecord and 12-end-of-file-appendices.md#12.7 PRIM-UserManual

AC2 (exact files, state tables, constraints, failure modes, verification evidence) -> exact files pinned in this MT scope.relevant_files and scope.constraints; legacy surfaces named in refinement.json refinement.spec_conflicts_requiring_enrichment SPEC-CONFLICT-002 (../wtc-kernel-005 model_manual/, model_manual_md_gen.rs, app/src-tauri/src/manual.rs); state tables: stub architecture_seed.data_model_seed (user_manual_pages); failure modes: stub failure_scenarios (legacy ModelManual naming confuses no-context models; migration breaks manual routes); verification evidence: refinement.json refinement.verification_results (4x PASS, 1x BLOCKED_PRE_EXISTING)

AC3 (sidecar research rejected, ideas restated Handshake-native) -> stub research_basis.normative_status=non_normative_context_only + translation_rule; UserManual duty restated as product law per GLOBAL-BUILD manual policy rather than external reference; this MT scope.constraints bar sidecar-paper and outside-runtime proof

AC4 (HBR rows named with concrete reasons) -> HBR-INT-001, HBR-MAN-001, HBR-SWARM-001 = NOT_APPLICABLE for runtime proof on this spec-seed record MT (no product surface, code, test, or runtime state changed by it; HBR-MAN requirement content is itself the subject recorded in 10-product-surfaces.md#10.15.8 and 12-end-of-file-appendices.md#12.7 for implementation MTs); CX-503R PostgreSQL-only storage law = SATISFIED at spec level by 02-system-architecture.md#2.3.13.11 and 11-shared-dev-platform-and-oss-foundations.md#9.1.1 which lock PostgreSQL/EventLedger-only authority and bar SQLite for WP-KERNEL-009 paths; per packet.json acceptance_matrix.note HBR proof is carried per-MT

concerns: Contract freshness skew: scope.relevant_files and the activation constraint pin the v02.191 bundle while SPEC_CURRENT and all evidence are v02.192 (contracts hydrated 07:20Z after the 06:45Z spec bump); validator should read the constraint intent (indexed bundle, not v02.182 monolith), but a hygiene pass updating contract pins to v02.192 would remove the ambiguity.
adversarial_review.refutation_attempts: Opened every cited artifact (refinement.json, spec-enrichment-proposal.json, stub contract, v02.192 spec modules 02/07/10/11/12, spec-changelog.jsonl, SPEC_CURRENT.md, packet.json); attacked criterion coverage AC1-AC4, citation validity, specificity, v02.192 freshness, and scope-artifact existence. Verified 10.15.8 verbatim: canonical UserManual concept, deterministic ModelManual->UserManualRecord mapping with compatibility receipts, the full UserManualRecord MUST-explain list, same-implementation-unit duty (build-rule defect), SPEC-CONFLICT-002 anchors, and user_manual_pages table.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
